ask_next.question: Where on the body is the wound?
ask_next.purpose: Location determines tourniquet eligibility and severity
ask_next.why: Neck/torso bleeds cannot be tourniqueted and are higher risk than limb bleeds

response_guides.note: Neck wound — gentle direct pressure only (no packing), call 911 immediately
Torso wound — maintain firm pressure, do not pack, call 911 immediately
Limb wound — tourniquet eligible if direct pressure fails after 5 minutes
Junctional wound — pack tightly with gauze, cannot tourniquet, call 911 immediately

ask_next.question: Is the blood bright red and pulsing, or darker and flowing more steadily?
ask_next.why: Arterial bleeds are more immediately life-threatening and need tourniquet faster

response_guides.note: Arterial bleed suspected — maximum urgency, apply tourniquet immediately for limb wounds

ask_next.question: Has the person lost consciousness, or are they dizzy and confused?
ask_next.why: Altered consciousness indicates significant blood loss — hemodynamic compromise

response_guides.note: Signs of hemorrhagic shock — call 911, lay flat, elevate legs, keep warm
